hawkish_phrases: monetary policy would need to be tightened somewhat earlier and by a somewhat greater extent over the forecast period than anticipated at the time of the November Report
a small margin of excess demand emerges by early 2020 and builds thereafter
inflation is projected to...remain above the 2% target in the second and third years
the UK economy has only a very limited degree of slack
firming of shorter-term measures of wage growth in recent quarters
dovish_phrases: Any future increases in Bank Rate are expected to be at a gradual pace and to a limited extent
Household consumption growth is expected to remain relatively subdued, reflecting weak real income growth
business investment...remains restrained by Brexit-related uncertainties
rates of productivity growth that are around half of their pre-crisis average
the Committee must balance any trade-off between the speed at which it intends to return inflation sustainably to the target and the support that monetary policy provides to jobs and activity
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.50%, but signaled that monetary policy would need to be tightened somewhat earlier and by a somewhat greater extent than previously anticipated, given excess demand is expected to emerge and inflation to remain above target.
